headline: In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
description: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

headline: Ending the war with Iran comes at the cost of major US concessions
Ukrainian drone assault on Moscow undermines Putin's promises of victory
JD Vance slams Israeli critics of US-Iran deal
description: The US vice president rebuked members of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's cabinet who have criticized the Iran deal, arguing that if he were in their position, 'I might not be attacking the only powerful ally that I have anywhere left in the entire world.'

headline: As heatwave hits, French government tries not to appear overwhelmed by climate crisis
description: According to the authorities, most measures in the national climate change adaptation plan are underway. However, critics say the plan lacks structure and adequate resources, as temperatures in France are expected to reach up to 40°C in the coming days.

headline: Knicks fans flood New York streets for championship parade
description: Confetti in the Knicks' blue and orange swirled through the air as thousands of people watched the parade roll up a 10-block route in lower Manhattan known as the 'Canyon of Heroes.'

headline: 'Incidents multiply in the Baltic Sea, where the Russian navy 'now operates in a completely overt manner'
description: After a Russian frigate fired warning shots at a civilian yacht in the English Channel on June 16, Rear Admiral Johan Norlén, chief of staff of the Swedish navy, describes an increasingly tense climate in Baltic waters.

headline: Judicial investigation opened against former French PM Edouard Philippe
Macron receives Trump at Château de Versailles to discuss Ukraine
Iran emerges in position of strength after ceasefire signed with US
2026 World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo, Luka Modric and Edin Dzeko, still in the game in their 40s
Jordan Bardella meets with far-right allies in Poland
Paris awards honorary citizenship to Palestinian civilians and journalists
Tebnine hospital bears witness to ongoing war in southern Lebanon
France shuts down clandestine Chinese 'police stations' operating on its territory
On Donbas front, warehouse manager aims to improve daily life for Ukrainian soldiers
Insee lowers growth forecasts as French households bear brunt of Middle East crisis
Serbian court jails school shooter's parents after high-profile retrial
French tanker crosses Strait of Hormuz days after US-Iran deal
French security replaces Palantir with domestic firm ChapsVision, a complicated choice
description: Used since the 2015 terrorist attacks, the controversial American company's data analysis software is set to be gradually replaced by that of a French provider. The long-awaited decision presents a significant technological challenge.

headline: French environment minister's absence from public debate underscores government's climate inaction
description: Appiointed in October 2025, Monique Barbut is struggling to make her mark. This situation goes beyond her as an individual and highlights how the government has abandoned the issue.

headline: In China's Qinhuangdao, architect Moshe Safdie's futuristic complex reimagines urban living
description: The utopian 'Hai Bi Tai,' a 16-story residential complex with 1,800 units, most of which offer views of the sky, the city and the Bohai Sea, still has half its apartments standing empty due to a real estate crisis.

headline: Economic and xenophobic: The double threat posed by the Rassemblement National
French government urged to reveal hidden assets of the rich
'The relationship with Washington that once underpinned Netanyahu's power has become a burden'
